purpose: Fix, harden and complete the native Ananta worker evolution toward a Hermes-like but Hub-governed agent executor. This track is about the Ananta worker itself, not about integrating external Hermes Agent.

review_basis.summary: The Ananta worker has moved toward a governed agent executor with native runtime, provider dispatch, worker contracts, context bundles, result memory and CodeCompass/RAG support. The main missing pieces are hard runtime enforcement, ToolInvocationEnvelope, provider-risk policy, SkillManifest/SkillRunner, memory policy, subworker delegation, and stronger end-to-end integration tests.

global_principles: Hub owns goals, tasks, policy, approval, context, routing, audit and durable state.
Native worker executes delegated work only and cannot mint authority for itself.
Every model call, tool call, command, memory write, provider dispatch and subworker spawn must be capability-gated.
Descriptor metadata is not enough; security-relevant rules must be enforced in runtime gates.
Every meaningful result must become a typed artifact with provenance and traceability.
If policy, approval, context or audit state is missing, fail closed or degrade safely.
Self-improvement is allowed only as proposals until reviewed and enabled by Hub/operator policy.
non_goals: Do not replace Hub policy with worker-local decisions.
Do not let worker profiles remain only documentation while runtime bypasses them.
Do not add unrestricted agent autonomy to the native worker.
Do not make MemoryEntryDB a free-form unsanitized dump of worker output.
Do not let provider fallback escalate from local to cloud without policy.
Do not implement hidden cron/subworker behavior inside the worker.
Do not make skills auto-enable themselves after generation.

milestones.title: Provider registry, model routing and cloud boundaries
Result memory and safe learning loop

tasks.title: Replace worker-local policy_decision_ref allow stubs with Hub-issued decisions
tasks.problem: NativeWorkerRuntimeService builds several request/result payloads with policy_decision_ref decision='allow' and policy_version='native_worker_runtime_v1'. That is acceptable for prototypes but dangerous as architecture: the worker must not invent policy allowance.
implementation_notes: Thread a real Hub policy decision object into prepare_native_command_plan and execute_and_verify_command.
If no Hub decision is available, return degraded/blocked according to requested_state_on_policy_denied.
Keep a dev/test-only compatibility wrapper if needed, but make it explicit and tested.
acceptance_criteria: NativeWorkerRuntimeService no longer creates synthetic allow policy decisions for command_plan or command_execute in normal runtime path.
prepare_native_command_plan requires policy_decision_ref or an explicit compatibility flag.
execute_and_verify_command requires policy_decision_ref or an explicit compatibility flag.
Missing policy decision returns blocked/degraded with reason_code='missing_policy_decision'.
Trace and artifacts include the Hub-issued policy decision id/version.
Tests prove command_execute cannot run when policy_decision_ref is missing.
test_requirements: Add missing policy decision negative test.
Add allowed Hub decision positive test.
Update existing native worker tests to pass explicit policy decision.
anti_patterns: Do not silently default to allow.
Do not hide policy decisions only in free-text output.

tasks.title: Add WorkerPreflightGate before every native worker action
tasks.problem: Worker profiles and contracts exist, but there should be one mandatory preflight gate before command planning, command execution, provider dispatch, memory write, skill run and subworker delegation.
implementation_notes: Create agent/services/worker_preflight_gate_service.py or worker/core/preflight_gate.py.
Gate input should include task_id, mode, capability_id, worker_profile, allowed_tools, policy_decision_ref, context_bundle_id, approval_ref and provider info when relevant.
Return allow, confirm_required, blocked or degraded with reason_code.
acceptance_criteria: Every native worker entrypoint calls WorkerPreflightGate before side effects.
Gate validates worker_profile, capability_id, allowed_tools, policy_decision_ref, context presence and approval requirements.
Gate denies unknown capability ids by default.
Gate denies mutation-capable actions when approval is required but missing.
Gate returns machine-readable reason codes such as missing_policy_decision, missing_capability, approval_required, tool_not_allowed, context_missing.
No command execution, provider dispatch, memory write or artifact apply happens before preflight allow.
test_requirements: Integration test proving command executor is not called when preflight denies.
anti_patterns: Do not duplicate preflight logic in each service.
Do not enforce capability only through prompt text.

tasks.title: Turn WorkerCapabilityProfiles from metadata into enforceable runtime rules
tasks.problem: WorkerCapabilityService defines profiles such as planner/coder/reviewer/operator with scopes, tool classes and limits, but these profiles must be enforced by runtime gates.
implementation_notes: Add helper resolve_worker_profile(profile_id) and evaluate_worker_profile_action(...).
Normalize profile aliases consistently.
Explicitly map allowed_scopes/tool_classes/limits to runtime decisions.
acceptance_criteria: planner/planning-agent cannot execute tools, persist plans, materialize tasks or route workers unless separately granted by Hub.
reviewer cannot mutate files or execute shell commands.
coder may propose changes but terminal/write actions remain policy/approval-gated.
operator admin-like actions require admin approval/audit.
Unknown profile downgrades to safest profile or denies, not coder/operator.
Profile decision appears in preflight trace.
test_requirements: Profile matrix tests for planner, reviewer, coder, operator.
anti_patterns: Do not treat profile labels as documentation only.
Do not let requested worker_profile from task context escalate privileges without Hub policy.

tasks.title: Normalize capability vocabulary across worker runtime, router and provider dispatch
tasks.problem: Capabilities are expressed in several vocabularies: worker.command.plan, worker.command.execute, patching, patch_propose, shell_execution, shell_execute, review, code_review. This creates policy gaps and confusing routing.
implementation_notes: Create one normalization helper for capability aliases.
Keep raw requested capability for trace, but compare canonical capability internally.
Document canonical names.
acceptance_criteria: shell_execute and shell_execution normalize to one canonical shell execution capability.
patching, patch_propose and patch_apply are distinct canonical capabilities with explicit risk levels.
review and code_review normalize or alias consistently.
worker.command.plan and worker.command.execute map to canonical planning/shell_execution classes for policy checks.
Unknown capability is denied or degraded, never implicitly treated as planning.
ToolRouter, WorkerPreflightGate and ProviderDispatch use the same normalization helper.
test_requirements: Regression test for command_execute not downgraded to harmless planning.
anti_patterns: Do not add aliases in one service only.
Do not collapse patch_propose and patch_apply into the same permission.

tasks.title: Add immutable CapabilitySnapshot per worker execution
tasks.problem: Worker executions should not read mutable config repeatedly and accidentally gain capability mid-run. Each execution needs an immutable snapshot derived from Hub policy and worker profile.
implementation_notes: Add CapabilitySnapshot model or dict schema.
Include capability ids, allowed_tools, denied_tools, policy_decision_ref, approval_refs, provider constraints and context scope.
Store snapshot hash in trace/artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Each worker job/execution stores a capability_snapshot or capability_snapshot_ref.
Runtime checks read from the snapshot rather than live mutable config after execution starts.
Snapshot hash is included in worker execution result metadata.
Attempted mid-run config change does not expand permissions of an active execution.
Tests prove snapshot is immutable for the execution.
test_requirements: Unit test for snapshot construction and hash stability.
Integration test for config change after job creation not expanding active permissions.
anti_patterns: Do not re-read broad global config before each tool call without snapshot boundary.
Do not let worker modify its own snapshot.

tasks.title: Add fail-closed behavior for missing audit pipeline on mutation actions
tasks.problem: Mutation-capable worker actions need audit. If audit append/persist is unavailable, executing anyway makes the system hard to trust.
implementation_notes: Define which actions are audit-critical: command_execute, patch_apply/write, provider_cloud_call, memory_write, subworker_spawn, skill_enable.
Fail closed for high-risk actions; allow degraded for read-only planning if policy allows.
acceptance_criteria: Audit-critical actions check audit availability or append result before proceeding according to policy.
If audit append fails for command_execute, action is blocked/degraded before command runs.
Read-only plan/review may proceed with degraded warning only if policy allows.
Audit failure reason is machine-readable.
Tests simulate audit append failure for command_execute and plan_only.
test_requirements: Audit failure negative test for command_execute.
anti_patterns: Do not make audit best-effort for high-risk mutations.

tasks.title: Create WorkerToolRegistry with explicit tool metadata
tasks.problem: allowed_tools currently appears as lists in worker contracts/jobs. A hard tool registry is needed so each tool has declared capabilities, risk, side effects, schemas and approval requirements.
implementation_notes: Registry entries should be data-first and inspectable.
Start with native command plan/execute, file read, patch propose, patch apply, test run, verification, provider call, memory write.
acceptance_criteria: WorkerToolRegistry exists and can list registered tools.
Each tool declares tool_id, capability_classes, risk_class, side_effect_type, input_schema, output_schema, requires_approval, audit_required and enabled state.
Unknown tool id is denied by default.
Registry metadata is available to WorkerPreflightGate and diagnostics.
Tests verify registry entries for native command plan/execute and memory write.
anti_patterns: Do not infer tool risk only from tool name at call time.
Do not expose arbitrary Python callables as tools automatically.

tasks.title: Create ToolInvocationEnvelope for every worker tool call
tasks.problem: Tool calls need a typed envelope that carries capability snapshot, context, approval, resource limits and audit correlation. Without it, tool execution remains scattered and hard to enforce.
implementation_notes: Use Pydantic or dataclass plus validation consistent with project style.
ToolInvocationEnvelope should be constructed after WorkerPreflightGate allow.
acceptance_criteria: ToolInvocationEnvelope includes execution_id, tool_id, task_id, capability_snapshot_ref/hash, context_refs, approval_ref, arguments, timeout_seconds, max_output_chars, audit_correlation_id and resource_limits.
Envelope validates argument shape before tool execution.
Envelope rejects unknown tool id.
Every native command execution path receives a ToolInvocationEnvelope or documented compatible wrapper.
ToolInvocationEnvelope hash appears in trace for command_execute.
test_requirements: Command execution integration test with envelope.
anti_patterns: Do not pass raw command strings directly into executor without invocation envelope.
Do not hide approval refs only in metadata.

tasks.title: Gate command planning and execution through WorkerToolRegistry
tasks.problem: Native command planning/execution currently has command classification and shell policy, but it must also be a registered tool invocation with explicit capability and approval checks.
implementation_notes: command_plan is lower risk and returns artifact only.
command_execute is high-risk and requires shell_execute capability plus approval where policy says so.
acceptance_criteria: command_plan uses tool_id='native.command.plan' or equivalent registered id.
command_execute uses tool_id='native.command.execute' or equivalent registered id.
command_execute denies when tool is disabled or missing from allowed_tools/capability snapshot.
approval_required commands are blocked without valid approval_ref.
Shell policy classification is recorded but cannot override Hub denial.
Tests prove denied command does not call subprocess.
anti_patterns: Do not let local shell allowlist override Hub policy denial.
Do not allow command_execute from planner/reviewer profile.

tasks.title: Add ToolResult contract with separated stdout/stderr/artifacts/side effects
tasks.problem: Native runtime returns dicts with output and artifact_refs. A common ToolResult contract would make tool outputs safer and easier to audit.
implementation_notes: ToolResult should avoid one giant free-text blob.
Keep compatibility mapping to current output fields.
acceptance_criteria: ToolResult includes status, exit_code, stdout_ref, stderr_ref, artifacts, warnings, side_effects, resource_usage, reason_code and sanitized_summary.
Command executor output maps into ToolResult before WorkerResult/native_runtime output.
Large stdout/stderr are bounded or artifact-referenced.
Side effects are explicit and empty for plan-only actions.
Tests verify large output truncation and side_effects list.
anti_patterns: Do not put all tool data into output string only.

tasks.title: Add resource limits per tool call
tasks.problem: Timeout exists for command execution, but tool calls should also have max output, file-touch, process and artifact limits.
implementation_notes: Start with timeout_seconds and max_output_chars.
Add file/process limits as metadata if enforcement is not yet practical.
acceptance_criteria: ToolInvocationEnvelope supports timeout_seconds, max_output_chars, max_artifact_bytes and max_files_touched fields.
Command execution enforces timeout_seconds and max_output_chars.
Exceeded output is truncated with explicit marker and original length metadata.
Resource limit breach returns degraded/failed with reason_code='resource_limit_exceeded'.
Tests cover timeout and max_output_chars.
anti_patterns: Do not inject unlimited command output into model or memory context.

tasks.title: Extend provider registry for LLM/model providers, not only worker execution providers
tasks.problem: GenericProviderRegistry and WorkerExecutorDispatchBridge are good for execution providers, but Hermes-like worker behavior also needs local/cloud LLM provider routing with risk, cost, credentials and health.
implementation_notes: Reuse GenericProviderRegistry where possible.
Add provider_family='llm_inference' descriptors for ollama, lmstudio, hosted_openai, openrouter/custom OpenAI-compatible if desired.
acceptance_criteria: Provider registry can list llm_inference descriptors separately from worker_execution descriptors.
Each LLM provider declares provider_id, location, local_or_cloud, credential_source, supports_tools, supports_streaming, context_limit, cost_class, risk_class and health probe.
Provider resolution never exposes API key values.
Unknown or disabled provider resolves degraded/disabled, not available.
Tests cover ollama/lmstudio local descriptors and hosted provider descriptor.
test_requirements: Provider registry tests for llm_inference family.
anti_patterns: Do not overload worker_execution provider family for model calls.
Do not store raw credentials in descriptors.

tasks.title: Add model/provider selection policy for native worker calls
tasks.problem: Hermes-like native worker will need helper model calls for planning, summarization, validation, compression and skill review. Provider selection must be Hub policy driven, not worker convenience driven.
implementation_notes: Use model_policy from ExecutionEnvelope/CapabilitySnapshot.
Worker may report provider availability but Hub/policy should decide allowed provider set.
acceptance_criteria: Native worker model call helper accepts allowed_providers, denied_providers, cloud_allowed, max_tokens and sensitivity constraints.
Cloud provider is denied when cloud_allowed=false.
Fallback provider is allowed only if within same or lower risk/cost class and policy permits fallback.
Provider selection result records selected provider, alternatives and rejection reasons.
Tests cover local-first selection, cloud-blocked selection and fallback denial.
anti_patterns: Do not try every configured provider until one works.
Do not fall back from local to cloud silently.

tasks.title: Isolate provider credentials from tool subprocesses and memory
tasks.problem: As provider support grows, credentials must not leak into command subprocess env, logs, artifacts or result memory.
implementation_notes: Use credential_source references, not raw values, in descriptors and traces.
Subprocess env should be minimal/allowlisted.
acceptance_criteria: Provider API keys are never serialized in provider descriptors, traces, artifacts or memory entries.
Command subprocesses do not inherit provider API keys by default.
Redaction catches Authorization headers, API key env vars and common provider token names.
Provider credential_source appears in diagnostics without secret value.
Tests prove fake API key is not present in command env/log/memory.
anti_patterns: Do not place provider secrets in generic agent_cfg passed to workers/tools.

tasks.title: Add provider health and degraded-state integration
tasks.problem: Provider registry resolution handles disabled/missing factory, but native worker diagnostics should expose health/degraded state consistently for execution and inference providers.
implementation_notes: Health probes must be cheap and no-secret.
Do not call expensive model generation as default health.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics list execution providers and LLM providers separately.
Each provider reports ready, disabled, degraded, unauthorized, unavailable or misconfigured.
Health payload includes provider_id, family, location, risk_class and reason_code.
Health payload never exposes credentials.
Unavailable optional provider does not fail whole system health unless required by active profile.
anti_patterns: Do not hide missing optional providers as generic startup failure.

tasks.title: Add provider provenance to all model-generated worker artifacts
tasks.problem: Artifacts generated using model/provider calls must record which provider/model/policy generated them for reproducibility and audit.
implementation_notes: Provider provenance should include provider_id/model/local_or_cloud and prompt/template hashes, not raw prompt by default.
acceptance_criteria: Every model-generated worker artifact includes provider_id, model_id, local_or_cloud, policy_decision_ref, prompt_hash, context_hash and generated_at.
Artifacts produced without model call explicitly mark provider='native_runtime' or equivalent.
Result memory preserves provider provenance refs, not raw secrets/prompts.
Tests verify provider provenance on plan/review/summary/patch proposal artifacts.
anti_patterns: Do not store raw full prompts just to preserve provenance by default.

tasks.title: Make CodeCompass/RAG context mandatory for code-aware worker modes
tasks.problem: The project has strong CodeCompass/RAG tests and ContextBundleService, but code-aware worker modes must fail/degrade if they cannot get grounded project context instead of guessing.
implementation_notes: Apply to task kinds coding, refactor, bugfix, review, architecture/code-analysis unless explicitly plan-only/no-context is requested.
Use required_context_scope and retrieval_intent.
acceptance_criteria: Code-aware worker job creation requests CodeCompass/RAG context bundle by default.
If retrieval is unavailable and task requires grounded context, job becomes degraded/context_unavailable unless policy permits weak mode.
Context bundle metadata records CodeCompass channels used: fts/vector/graph/hybrid where applicable.
Worker prompt/context includes provenance and why_this_context metadata.
Tests prove coding task does not proceed with empty ungrounded context in strict mode.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker answer code questions from memory only when current code context is required.
Do not silently downgrade to generic LLM mode in strict governance.

tasks.title: Add ContextEnvelope compatibility layer around ContextBundleDB
tasks.problem: ContextBundleDB carries useful fields, but worker-runtime enforcement should use a typed ContextEnvelope with source refs, sensitivity, token budget, provenance and context hash.
implementation_notes: Do not replace existing DB model immediately; wrap it.
Build ContextEnvelope from ContextBundleDB and context_policy.
acceptance_criteria: ContextEnvelope model includes context_bundle_id, context_hash, blocks, total_tokens, sensitivity_summary, provenance, policy_version and retrieval_trace_ref.
ContextBundleDB can be converted to ContextEnvelope deterministically.
Each context block includes source_type, origin_id, sensitivity, estimated_tokens, content_ref/content_excerpt and provenance metadata.
ContextEnvelope validation rejects missing context_hash or unbounded raw all-files context.
Worker contracts can reference ContextEnvelope instead of loose context dict where practical.
anti_patterns: Do not pass giant context_text without block-level provenance into worker runtime.

tasks.title: Enforce context budget and output reserve before model/provider calls
tasks.problem: ContextBundleService has budget logic, but every worker model/provider call must explicitly enforce input budget and output reserve before sending context.
implementation_notes: Use budget sections from ContextBundleService.
Fail or compact before provider call, not after provider rejects context.
acceptance_criteria: Worker model-call builder receives total context limit and output reserve.
Input context is compacted/truncated before provider call if over budget.
Critical policy/approval/control instructions are never silently dropped.
Budget accounting appears in trace: requested_tokens, selected_tokens, dropped_tokens, output_reserve.
Tests cover compact, standard and full modes.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on provider context-window errors as normal budget control.

tasks.title: Strengthen context policy filtering for local vs cloud workers
tasks.problem: Context policy must prevent sensitive/project-private data from being sent to cloud providers or external workers unless explicitly allowed.
implementation_notes: Use llm_scope/local/cloud/external_worker classifications.
Apply filtering before provider call and before memory persist where needed.
acceptance_criteria: Context blocks have normalized sensitivity and allowed_llm_scope fields.
Cloud/external worker context builder excludes blocks not allowed for that scope.
Excluded blocks are recorded with reason code, not silently hidden.
Strict governance defaults to local-only for sensitive code/project context.
Tests prove secret/confidential/internal blocks are excluded from cloud context.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker or LLM decide whether a sensitive block is okay for cloud.

tasks.title: Add grounded prompt assembly contract for native worker
tasks.problem: CodeCompass grounded prompt assembly exists in tests, but native worker should have a stable contract for how task, policy, context, artifacts and output schema are assembled.
implementation_notes: Separate control instructions from untrusted context.
Mark context as data, not instructions.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt assembly output has sections: control, task, policy constraints, allowed tools, context, expected artifacts, output schema.
Untrusted retrieved context is clearly delimited and cannot override control instructions.
Prompt assembly includes context_hash and retrieval_trace_ref.
Prompt assembly excludes secrets according to context policy.
Tests snapshot a representative grounded prompt without secrets.
anti_patterns: Do not put untrusted context before control/policy instructions.

tasks.title: Add MemoryPolicy before ResultMemoryService persists worker output
tasks.problem: ResultMemoryService persistently stores compacted worker output. It needs explicit policy for what can be remembered, with redaction and sensitivity handling.
implementation_notes: Create normalize_memory_policy with allow_raw_history, max_chars, sensitivity, ttl, allowed_scopes, redact_before_persist.
Default should be safe: redacted compact memory, no raw history.
acceptance_criteria: ResultMemoryService evaluates MemoryPolicy before saving any MemoryEntryDB.
Secrets are redacted before content, summary, metadata and raw_history persist.
Raw output archive remains disabled by default.
Policy can disable memory write entirely for sensitive tasks.
Memory entry metadata records policy_version and redaction_applied.
test_requirements: Secret redaction before memory save test.
anti_patterns: Do not save full raw worker output into memory by default.

tasks.title: Separate session memory, project memory and proposed long-term memory
tasks.problem: Hermes-like memory should not mean one global memory bucket. Worker results need scoped memory types with different trust and approval rules.
implementation_notes: Add entry_scope or memory_scope if schema supports it; otherwise store in metadata as migration step.
Long-term/project-wide memory should be proposal-first for high-risk content.
acceptance_criteria: Memory entries distinguish session, task, project and proposed_long_term scopes.
Default worker result memory is task/project scoped only according to policy.
Long-term memory writes require explicit capability or are stored as MemoryProposalArtifact.
Retrieval can filter by memory scope.
Tests verify cross-project memory is not returned unintentionally.
test_requirements: Memory scope tests and retrieval filter tests.
anti_patterns: Do not let every successful worker output become global long-term memory.

tasks.title: Add MemoryProposalArtifact for self-improvement and durable lessons
tasks.problem: Native worker should be able to suggest reusable lessons/skills/memory, but not directly promote them into durable truth.
implementation_notes: Use for lessons learned, recurring failure patterns, useful project facts and possible skill proposals.
acceptance_criteria: MemoryProposalArtifact schema/type exists with title, rationale, evidence_refs, proposed_scope, sensitivity, confidence and approval_required.
Worker can emit memory proposals without writing long-term memory directly.
Approval/promotion path is separate and Hub-owned.
Rejected proposals do not appear in normal retrieval as trusted memory.
Tests cover proposal creation and non-promotion by default.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker self-promote memories into trusted global state.

tasks.title: Add memory retrieval provenance and trust scoring
tasks.problem: Result memory can be useful but must be treated as evidence with provenance and trust, not guaranteed truth.
implementation_notes: Start simple: trust_source, status, artifact refs, generated_by, approved flag.
acceptance_criteria: Memory retrieval chunks include memory_scope, generated_by, artifact_refs, confidence/trust metadata and approved flag where available.
Unapproved proposed memory is marked untrusted or excluded depending on policy.
Context bundle explainability exposes memory provenance.
Tests verify unapproved memory cannot silently override current code context.
anti_patterns: Do not mix generated summaries with source code facts without provenance.

tasks.title: Add memory cleanup/TTL policy
tasks.problem: Worker memory will grow and may retain sensitive or stale details. It needs cleanup and TTL policy.
implementation_notes: Start with metadata fields even if cleanup job is later.
acceptance_criteria: Memory entries can store ttl_seconds/expires_at or retention_class.
Default retention can differ for session/task/project memory.
Cleanup service can mark or remove expired entries safely.
Expired memory is excluded from retrieval unless admin/debug mode explicitly includes it.
Tests cover expired memory exclusion.
anti_patterns: Do not keep all worker output forever by default.

tasks.title: Create SkillManifest schema for native Ananta worker
tasks.problem: Hermes-like behavior requires reusable skills, but Ananta skills must be versioned, capability-bound and approval-aware. No clear SkillManifest/SkillRegistry implementation is visible yet.
implementation_notes: Keep first schema simple and strict.
Skills should be instructions/workflows plus allowed tool references, not arbitrary executable code by default.
acceptance_criteria: SkillManifest includes id, version, name, description, input_schema, output_schema, required_capabilities, allowed_tools, denied_tools, context_requirements, risk_class, approval_requirements, tests and owner/source.
Invalid manifest is rejected with reason_code='skill_manifest_invalid'.
Unknown required capability denies skill load/enable.
Skill risk class cannot be lower than risk implied by required tools/capabilities.
Schema has positive and negative fixtures.
anti_patterns: Do not store skills only as free-form prompt snippets without metadata.

tasks.title: Create SkillRegistry with disabled-by-default loading
tasks.problem: Skills need a registry with hash/version state and explicit enablement. Auto-loaded skills are a security risk.
implementation_notes: Registry can load manifests from configured directory but should not enable unsafe skills automatically.
Record content_hash for each skill.
acceptance_criteria: SkillRegistry lists available skills with id, version, enabled, risk_class, required_capabilities and content_hash.
Skills are disabled by default unless config/policy enables them.
Changing skill file changes content_hash.
Duplicate skill id/version is rejected or degraded with clear reason.
Diagnostics can list skills without exposing secrets or long prompts.
anti_patterns: Do not auto-enable newly generated skills.

tasks.title: Create SkillRunner through WorkerPreflightGate and ToolInvocationEnvelope
tasks.problem: Executing a skill must not bypass policy, allowed tools, context scope, provider policy or artifact enforcement.
implementation_notes: SkillRunner should receive ExecutionEnvelope/CapabilitySnapshot.
Skill steps call tools only through WorkerToolRegistry.
acceptance_criteria: SkillRunner refuses to run disabled or invalid skills.
SkillRunner checks required capabilities against CapabilitySnapshot before execution.
Skill tool calls go through ToolInvocationEnvelope and WorkerToolRegistry.
Skill output is converted into typed artifact(s).
Skill cannot perform memory_write, file_write, shell_execute or provider_cloud_call unless explicitly granted.
Tests prove malicious skill manifest cannot escape denied tools.
anti_patterns: Do not execute skill-defined Python code directly without sandbox/policy.

tasks.title: Add SkillProposalArtifact instead of direct self-modifying skills
tasks.problem: Hermes-like self-improvement should not mean the worker writes and enables its own skills. It should propose skills for review.
implementation_notes: Skill proposals can be generated from repeated task patterns or failures.
Install/enable is separate Hub/operator workflow.
acceptance_criteria: SkillProposalArtifact includes proposed SkillManifest, rationale, evidence_refs, expected_tests, risk_analysis and required_capabilities.
Worker can emit SkillProposalArtifact only with skill_propose capability.
Proposal does not enable or write active skill by default.
Approval workflow is referenced but can be deferred if not implemented.
Tests prove proposal creation does not mutate active registry.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker auto-install generated skill code.

tasks.title: Seed minimal safe native skills
tasks.problem: Once SkillRunner exists, useful baseline skills should be provided without dangerous powers.
implementation_notes: Start read-only and proposal-only.
No baseline skill should require unrestricted shell or cloud.
acceptance_criteria: Baseline skills include repo_context_review, test_failure_triage, patch_plan, security_review and result_summary.
Each baseline skill has manifest, fixture input and expected artifact output.
Baseline skills declare minimal required capabilities.
No baseline skill can execute shell or apply patches by default.
Skill tests pass without cloud provider/API key.
anti_patterns: Do not seed powerful mutation skills before SkillRunner gates are complete.

tasks.title: Create SubworkerEnvelope with capability subset enforcement
tasks.problem: WorkerJobService supports delegated worker jobs, but Hermes-like subworkers need an explicit envelope proving child authority is a subset of parent authority.
implementation_notes: SubworkerEnvelope should be Hub-created or Hub-approved.
Parent worker should not spawn arbitrary child workers locally.
acceptance_criteria: SubworkerEnvelope includes parent_execution_id, child_task_id, delegated_objective, reduced_capability_snapshot, context_subset_ref, timeout, max_depth, audit_correlation_id and expected_artifacts.
Child capability set must be subset of parent capability set unless Hub explicitly grants a new decision.
Child context scope must be subset or approved derived context.
Invalid subset returns denied/subworker_capability_escalation.
Tests cover subset allow and escalation deny.
anti_patterns: Do not let parent worker grant capabilities it does not have.

tasks.title: Add DelegationArtifact and execution tree trace
tasks.problem: Delegated work should be reconstructable as a tree of tasks/results/artifacts.
implementation_notes: A parent WorkerResult should reference child WorkerResults via DelegationArtifact.
acceptance_criteria: DelegationArtifact includes parent_execution_id, child_execution_ids, child_task_ids, delegated_capabilities, context_refs, statuses, artifact_refs and trace_refs.
WorkerJobService records delegation relationships.
Trace can reconstruct parent-child execution tree.
Failed child result does not disappear from parent summary.
Tests verify delegation artifact creation and trace tree.
anti_patterns: Do not flatten child results into one summary without trace refs.

tasks.title: Limit subworker recursion, fan-out and mutation concurrency
tasks.problem: Subworkers can create loops, runaway cost or conflicting mutations unless depth/fan-out/concurrency are enforced.
implementation_notes: Start with static limits: max_depth, max_children, max_parallel_mutations=0/1.
acceptance_criteria: SubworkerEnvelope enforces max_depth.
WorkerJobService enforces max_children per parent execution.
Parallel read-only delegation may be allowed, but mutation-capable delegation is serialized or blocked by default.
Cycle/self-delegation beyond limit returns denied/delegation_cycle_or_depth_limit.
Tests cover depth, fan-out and mutation concurrency limits.
anti_patterns: Do not allow recursive autonomous worker spawning without Hub control.

tasks.title: Add cancellation and timeout propagation across worker/subworker jobs
tasks.problem: Long-running worker/subworker executions need cancellation and timeout propagation to remain controllable.
implementation_notes: Tie into existing task lifecycle/cancel mechanisms if present.
acceptance_criteria: Cancelling parent worker job marks pending child jobs cancelled or cancel_requested.
Timeout on parent propagates to child jobs.
Running command/tool receives best-effort interrupt where supported.
Final result distinguishes cancelled, timeout and failed.
Trace records which side effects completed before cancellation.
anti_patterns: Do not leave orphan worker jobs after parent cancellation.

tasks.title: Create unified WorkerResult v2 for native worker paths
tasks.problem: Different services return dicts, WorkerResultDB, WorkerExecutionProviderResult and native_runtime payloads. A unified WorkerResult v2 is needed for reliable artifact-first flow.
implementation_notes: Do not break existing DB shape immediately; add mapper/compatibility layer.
Use statuses success/partial_success/denied/needs_approval/failed/degraded/cancelled/timeout.
acceptance_criteria: WorkerResult v2 includes status, reason_code, summary, artifacts, trace_bundle_ref, warnings, degraded_state, policy_observations, no_side_effects_confirmed and follow_up_tasks.
Native command plan/execute/verify outputs can map to WorkerResult v2.
Provider dispatch results can map to WorkerResult v2.
WorkerResultDB stores enough metadata to reconstruct WorkerResult v2 or references it.
Tests cover mapping from native runtime and provider result.
anti_patterns: Do not return only free-text output for non-trivial worker actions.

tasks.title: Add TraceBundle v2 for native worker execution
tasks.problem: Native worker execution has trace_id and context_hash, but Hermes-like governed executor needs structured trace bundles for model calls, tool calls, provider dispatch, memory writes, skills and subworkers.
implementation_notes: TraceBundle should be secret-free by default.
Use refs/hashes for prompts and artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: TraceBundle v2 includes execution_id, task_id, goal_id, worker_profile, capability_snapshot_hash, context_hash, policy_decision_ref, approval_refs, provider_calls, tool_calls, memory_writes, artifacts, timings, warnings and degraded states.
TraceBundle exists for success, denial, failure, timeout and cancellation.
TraceBundle records no raw secrets or full unredacted prompts by default.
Native command execute produces TraceBundle v2 or compatible ref.
Tests verify trace consistency across command plan -> execute -> verify.
anti_patterns: Do not scatter trace details only across logs and output strings.

tasks.title: Require typed artifacts for plan, command, test, verification, memory and skill outputs
tasks.problem: Native runtime already creates command/test/verification artifacts, but this artifact-first rule should apply uniformly to all worker modes.
implementation_notes: Add artifact type registry entries as needed.
No successful non-trivial worker result without artifact refs.
acceptance_criteria: command_plan outputs CommandPlanArtifact.
command_execute outputs TestResultArtifact or CommandResultArtifact plus VerificationArtifact.
memory proposal outputs MemoryProposalArtifact.
skill proposal outputs SkillProposalArtifact.
subworker delegation outputs DelegationArtifact.
WorkerResult success for non-trivial modes requires at least one typed artifact.
anti_patterns: Do not treat stdout as the only artifact.

tasks.title: Add worker diagnostics read model
tasks.problem: Operators need one view of native worker readiness: profiles, tools, providers, skills, memory, context/RAG, degraded state and policy gates.
implementation_notes: Make it safe for UI/TUI/dashboard.
No secrets, no raw prompts.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics include native_worker_enabled, worker_profiles, tool_registry_summary, provider_summary, skill_registry_summary, memory_policy_summary, context_policy_summary and last_degraded_reasons.
Diagnostics include whether hard enforcement gates are active.
Diagnostics never expose provider keys or secret context.
Doctor/status command can display concise worker readiness.
Tests verify redaction and expected fields.
anti_patterns: Do not expose raw env/config secrets in diagnostics.

tasks.title: Emit audit events for all sensitive worker transitions
tasks.problem: Audit must cover more than command execution: policy decision, provider call, memory write, skill run, skill proposal, subworker spawn and artifact persistence all matter.
implementation_notes: Use stable event names and reason codes.
Mutation or external/cloud events should be audit-critical.
acceptance_criteria: Audit events emitted for worker_preflight_decision, worker_tool_invocation, worker_provider_call, worker_memory_write, worker_skill_run, worker_skill_proposal, worker_subworker_spawn, worker_artifact_persisted and worker_result_recorded.
Events include task_id, goal_id, execution_id, policy_decision_ref, capability_snapshot_hash and reason_code where applicable.
Events exclude raw secrets and full prompts.
Audit failure behavior follows AWF-T006.
Tests verify audit events for success and denial.
anti_patterns: Do not log only success events. Denials are important audit data.

tasks.title: Add end-to-end native worker safe plan/review flow test
tasks.problem: Need a full flow test proving native worker can receive a task, get context, pass preflight, produce artifact, record trace and result memory without mutation.
implementation_notes: No cloud/API key.
Use mock provider/model if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Test creates or simulates code-aware task.
ContextBundle/ContextEnvelope is created with provenance.
WorkerPreflightGate allows read-only plan/review mode.
WorkerResult v2 contains typed PlanArtifact or ReviewArtifact.
TraceBundle v2 exists and includes context_hash/capability_snapshot_hash.
No workspace mutation occurs.
anti_patterns: Do not rely only on unit tests for worker architecture.

tasks.title: Add end-to-end native command execute denial and approval tests
tasks.problem: Command execution is the highest-risk native worker path and must prove denial/approval behavior end-to-end.
implementation_notes: Monkeypatch subprocess to detect accidental execution on denied path.
acceptance_criteria: command_execute without policy decision is denied and subprocess is not called.
command_execute without required approval is denied/needs_approval and subprocess is not called.
dangerous command is denied even if prompt asks to proceed.
approved safe command runs only inside workspace scope.
Trace/audit exist for denied and allowed paths.
anti_patterns: Do not test only command classifier without runtime path.

tasks.title: Add provider fallback and cloud-block integration tests
tasks.problem: Provider flexibility is Hermes-like but dangerous without tests proving cloud/local boundaries and fallback limits.
implementation_notes: Use fake providers only.
acceptance_criteria: Local provider selected when local available and task/context sensitive.
Cloud provider denied when cloud_allowed=false.
Fallback from local to cloud denied unless explicit policy allows it.
Provider credentials do not appear in traces/artifacts/logs.
Unavailable provider returns degraded with reason_code, not crash.
anti_patterns: Do not require live OpenAI/Ollama Cloud for tests.

tasks.title: Add worker security regression suite grouping
tasks.problem: Security tests exist across many files. A grouped worker security suite makes release gates and smaller models easier to use.
implementation_notes: Can import/reuse helpers from existing tests.
No need to duplicate all tests; provide focused high-value regression paths.
acceptance_criteria: Suite covers policy missing, approval missing, tool denied, provider cloud blocked, memory redaction, subworker escalation, skill unsafe manifest and context sensitivity.
Suite runs without external services/API keys.
Each test has a comment naming the bypass class it prevents.
Docs list command to run the worker security subset.
Release gate can reference this suite.
anti_patterns: Do not hide security tests only inside broad slow E2E suite.

tasks.title: Update native worker docs and this track after implementation
tasks.problem: The native worker architecture has evolved fast. Docs need to reflect real enforcement status, not planned behavior.
implementation_notes: Document current state, not aspirational state.
Use explicit 'implemented', 'partial', 'planned' sections.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain native worker runtime pipeline and enforcement gates.
Docs list which Hermes-like capabilities are implemented: provider dispatch, context, memory, skills, subworkers, tool registry.
Docs list limitations and default-deny behavior.
Docs include operator/developer test commands.
This JSON track is updated with done/blocked status and evidence once fixes are implemented.
JSON remains valid after updates.
test_requirements: json.loads todo.ananta-worker-fixup.json succeeds; docs commands reviewed.
anti_patterns: Do not mark planned skill/subworker features as already active.

definition_of_done_global: Native worker no longer invents allow policy decisions in normal runtime path.
WorkerCapabilityProfiles are enforced at runtime, not merely displayed.
WorkerToolRegistry and ToolInvocationEnvelope exist and protect command execution.
Provider registry supports execution and LLM/model provider families with local/cloud policy.
Provider credentials are isolated from subprocesses, traces, artifacts and memory.
Code-aware worker modes require grounded CodeCompass/RAG context or degrade safely.
ContextEnvelope wraps context bundles with provenance, sensitivity and budget accounting.
Result memory is policy-gated, redacted and scoped.
Skill system is manifest/registry/runner based and cannot self-enable.
Subworker delegation uses capability-subset envelopes and traceable artifacts.
WorkerResult v2 and TraceBundle v2 cover native worker outputs.
Security regression tests prove policy, tool, provider, memory, skill and subworker bypasses are blocked.
summary_notes: This track is about making the native Ananta worker more Hermes-like while preserving Ananta's stronger security model.
The highest priority is not more autonomy; it is hard enforcement: policy gates, tool envelopes, provider boundaries, context envelopes and audited artifacts.
Skills, subworkers and self-improvement should be added only after the minimum safe fix slice is implemented.
